Company.title: Company
Company.description: Represents the single Italian company (SRL) using the application, storing its fiscal and contact information required for invoicing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Company entity.

companyName.description: The official registered name of the company.

vatNumber.description: The company's VAT (Value Added Tax) number, mandatory for Italian invoicing.

taxCode.description: The company's fiscal code (Codice Fiscale) in Italy.

address.description: The street address of the company.

city.description: The city where the company is located.

province.description: The province where the company is located (e.g., RM for Rome).

zip.description: The postal code of the company's address.

country.description: The country where the company is registered, typically 'IT' for Italy.

pecEmail.description: The company's Certified Electronic Mail (PEC) address, used for official communications in Italy.

iban.description: The International Bank Account Number (IBAN) of the company's bank account.

fiscalRegime.description: The company's fiscal regime (Regime Fiscale) according to Italian tax law, e.g., 'RF01' for ordinary.

Client.title: Client
Client.description: Represents a client to whom the company issues invoices, storing their relevant fiscal and contact details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Client entity.

name.description: The full name or legal name of the client.

vatNumber.description: The client's VAT number, if applicable. Required for electronic invoicing to B2B clients.

taxCode.description: The client's fiscal code (Codice Fiscale) in Italy, used if VAT number is not present or for B2C clients.

address.description: The street address of the client.

city.description: The city where the client is located.

province.description: The province where the client is located (e.g., MI for Milan).

zip.description: The postal code of the client's address.

country.description: The country where the client is registered.

pec.description: The client's Certified Electronic Mail (PEC) address, used for official communications in Italy, or standard email if PEC is not available.

sdiCode.description: The 7-character 'Sistema di Interscambio' (SDI) code for the client, mandatory for electronic invoicing in Italy.

Invoice.title: Invoice
Invoice.description: Represents a generated invoice, including its header details, financial summaries, and references to associated files and client.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Invoice entity.

number.description: The sequential number of the invoice for the given year (e.g., '1' for 1/2026).

year.description: The fiscal year to which the invoice belongs (e.g., 2026).

date.description: The date the invoice was issued.

subtotal.description: The sum of all invoice item line totals before VAT is applied.

vatTotal.description: The total amount of VAT applied to the invoice.

total.description: The grand total amount of the invoice, including subtotal and VAT.

status.description: The current status of the invoice (e.g., 'draft', 'issued', 'paid').

xmlUrl.description: URL to the generated FatturaPA XML file stored in Firebase Storage.

pdfUrl.description: URL to the generated courtesy PDF file stored in Firebase Storage.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the invoice record was created.

InvoiceItem.title: InvoiceItem
InvoiceItem.description: Represents a single line item within an invoice, detailing the product or service, quantity, price, and VAT.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InvoiceItem entity.

description.description: A description of the product or service for this invoice line item.

quantity.description: The quantity of the product or service.

unitPrice.description: The price per unit of the product or service.

vatRate.description: The VAT (Value Added Tax) rate applicable to this line item (e.g., 4, 5, 10, 22).

lineTotal.description: The total amount for this line item (quantity * unitPrice) before VAT.

definition.description: Represents the single Italian company using the application. This collection will typically contain only one document (e.g., 'main_company').

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the company.

definition.description: Stores fiscal and contact details for clients. Each document includes a denormalized 'companyId' field for explicit ownership and authorization independence.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique identifier for the client.

definition.description: Stores details for generated invoices. Each document includes 'clientId' and a denormalized 'companyId' field for explicit ownership and authorization independence.

params.name: invoiceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the invoice.

definition.description: Stores individual line items for an invoice. Each document includes 'invoiceId' and a denormalized 'companyId' for explicit ownership and authorization independence.

params.name: invoiceItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the invoice item.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for FatturaNow is designed to support a single Italian company, prioritizing security, scalability, and debuggability as per the core design principles.

Authorization Independence & Denormalization:
All core business entities (Client, Invoice, InvoiceItem) are structured in top-level collections as requested. To strictly adhere to the "Authorization Independence via Denormalization" mandate, each document within these collections will explicitly include a `companyId` field. For this single-company application, this `companyId` will be a consistent identifier (e.g., "main_company") across all documents. This denormalization ensures that security rules can determine a document's ownership (`companyId`) directly from the document itself, without requiring `get()` operations to parent documents or external collections. This allows for atomic write operations and simplifies rule evaluation, making authorization robust and easy to debug.

Structural Segregation & QAPs:
Each collection (/company, /clients, /invoices, /invoiceItems) maintains a homogeneous security posture. All documents within /clients, /invoices, and /invoiceItems implicitly belong to the single company and are accessible by authorized administrators. This clear segregation, combined with the denormalized `companyId` on each document, enables secure `list` operations. Security rules will simply check if the authenticated user (`request.auth.uid`) is an authorized administrator for the single `companyId` (e.g., by checking for existence in a `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection). Since `companyId` is present in every document, a `list` query will inherently only return documents owned by the single, authorized company, without requiring expensive or insecure filters within the rules themselves.

Access Modeling (DBAC):
Authorization relies on `request.auth.uid`. For administrator roles, the "Existence over Content" pattern is employed. A dedicated collection, `/roles_admin/{uid}`, will contain documents where the ID is the `uid` of an administrator. Security rules will grant access if `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))` is true. This ensures role-based access is managed within the database, fulfilling DBAC requirements without relying on custom claims. (Note: `roles_admin` is not listed in `firestorePaths` due to schema constraints requiring a `$ref` to a defined entity.)

Hierarchical Paths for User-Owned Data (Implicitly):
While the application is single-tenant, the structure implicitly follows hierarchical ownership. The entire application's data is conceptually "owned" by the single `Company` document. Although Client, Invoice, InvoiceItem are top-level collections, the explicit `companyId` field in each document provides the necessary ownership context, aligning with the principle of clear ownership for future scalability. InvoiceItem documents are linked to Invoice documents via the `invoiceId` field.

Data Clarity and Predictability:
The structure adheres to predictable schemas and explicit state modeling, such as the `status` field in Invoice, enhancing debuggability and maintainability. Semantic naming conventions are consistently applied (e.g., `companyId`, `clientId`, `invoiceId`).

This design creates a secure, efficient, and maintainable Firestore foundation for FatturaNow, ready for its current single-company scope and adaptable for potential future extensions.
